title: CySA+ Practice Questions
summary: 15 medium/hard CySA+ practice questions covering vulnerability validation, OAuth abuse, PowerShell triage, Kerberoasting, EDR investigation, SIEM tuning, Linux privilege escalation, IDOR, C2 beaconing, ransomware containment, threat intelligence, CASB/DLP, DNS tunneling, evidence handling, and compensating controls.

questions.question:
A vulnerability scanner reports that an internal web application is vulnerable to a high-severity authentication bypass. The application is only reachable through the VPN, but the VPN is used by contractors and third-party support staff.

What should the analyst do FIRST?
options: Lower the severity because the application is not internet-facing
Validate exploitability and identify who can reach the affected application
Accept the risk because authenticated VPN access is required
Disable the vulnerability scanner rule until the next maintenance window

questions.question:
A SOC analyst sees the following sequence:

- User receives a Microsoft 365 phishing email
- User grants consent to an unverified OAuth application
- Mailbox searches occur for "invoice", "payment", and "wire"
- No new sign-in occurs after the password reset

What is the MOST likely reason suspicious access continues?
options: The attacker is using previously granted OAuth permissions or active sessions
The attacker is performing DNS tunneling through Exchange Online
The password reset failed because the domain controller was offline
The mailbox searches are caused by normal Outlook indexing

questions.question:
A Windows endpoint alert shows:

Parent: explorer.exe
Process: powershell.exe
CommandLine: powershell.exe -NoProfile -EncodedCommand ...
Network: 198.51.100.24:443

The user says they manually opened PowerShell to troubleshoot printer mapping.

Which evidence would BEST help determine whether the activity is suspicious?
options: Whether the user has previously opened PowerShell for printer troubleshooting
Whether the encoded command decodes to expected admin activity or malicious behavior
Whether the workstation has a DHCP lease from the correct network
Whether the destination IP responds to ICMP ping requests

questions.question:
A threat hunter sees multiple Kerberos service ticket requests for different SPNs from one workstation. The tickets use RC4 encryption, but there are no failed logins and the user authenticated normally.

What should the hunter suspect?
options: Kerberoasting activity may be occurring despite normal authentication
Password spraying is confirmed because RC4 is always a bad password error
Golden ticket usage is confirmed because all SPNs are domain controllers
DNS tunneling is likely because Kerberos always uses TXT records

questions.question:
An EDR product detects a suspicious binary on one host. The hash is unknown, the binary is unsigned, it was created in C:\Users\Public, and it was executed by rundll32.exe.

Which action is MOST appropriate FIRST?
options: Immediately wipe all systems that have rundll32.exe
Isolate the affected host and collect process, file, and network artifacts
Ignore the alert because rundll32.exe is a legitimate Windows binary
Delete the file first so it cannot be collected by another process

questions.question:
A company receives many SIEM alerts from impossible travel detection. Investigation shows most are caused by corporate VPN exit nodes and mobile carrier routing.

Which improvement BEST reduces false positives without disabling the detection?
options: Add known VPN/mobile egress context and require additional suspicious signals
Disable all geographic anomaly detections for privileged users
Lower log retention so repeated alerts age out faster
Block every login that uses a mobile network provider

questions.question:
A Linux server shows:

sudo: appuser : USER=root ; COMMAND=/usr/bin/chmod +s /bin/bash

Why is this concerning?
options: It can create a repeated privilege escalation path using an SUID shell
It rotates the root password and forces all sessions to reauthenticate
It enables encrypted DNS tunneling through the bash binary
It deletes the bash executable and prevents shell access permanently

questions.question:
A web server log shows repeated requests:

GET /api/orders/1001
GET /api/orders/1002
GET /api/orders/1003

The same authenticated user receives HTTP 200 responses for orders belonging to other users.

Which vulnerability is MOST likely?
options: Reflected XSS
SQL injection
IDOR
Path traversal

questions.question:
A malware alert is triggered by traffic to a newly registered domain over HTTPS every 60 seconds. The payload size is small and consistent. No large outbound transfer is observed.

Which behavior is MOST likely?
options: Command-and-control beaconing
Bulk data exfiltration
DHCP address renewal
Kerberos pre-authentication failure

questions.question:
During a ransomware incident, a manager asks the SOC to restore from backups immediately. The infected host still has mapped network drives and is actively encrypting files.

What should happen FIRST?
options: Restore backups immediately to reduce downtime
Isolate the infected host to stop spread and preserve evidence
Delete all encrypted files before confirming backup status
Reconnect mapped drives to identify which files are affected

questions.question:
A SOC receives a threat intelligence feed with IP addresses from an adversary campaign. Many IPs are cloud-hosted and change frequently.

What would be MORE useful for long-term detection?
options: TTPs, tool behavior, infrastructure patterns, and detection logic
The exact IP list only, because cloud IPs rarely change
A screenshot of the threat actor's public website
The number of followers the threat actor has on social media

questions.question:
A security engineer wants to monitor whether sensitive files are uploaded to personal cloud storage services from managed endpoints.

Which control would BEST help detect and enforce this policy?
options: CASB or DLP controls integrated with endpoint/cloud activity
NTP synchronization on all domain controllers
RAID mirroring on the file server
Port security on unused switch ports only

questions.question:
A host makes thousands of DNS TXT queries with long high-entropy subdomains. The domain is newly registered, and there is no matching browser activity.

Which next step is MOST appropriate?
options: Treat as possible DNS tunneling and identify the process and user behind the queries
Treat as normal Active Directory discovery because TXT records are always internal
Ignore the activity because DNS is required for normal internet browsing
Delete DNS logs first to reduce storage during the investigation

questions.question:
An analyst is preparing evidence for possible legal action after a confirmed data theft incident.

Which action is MOST important?
options: Maintain chain of custody and preserve original evidence integrity
Edit log timestamps so the timeline is easier to understand
Share the raw evidence with all employees for transparency
Reboot all systems before collecting volatile evidence

questions.question:
A system owner refuses to patch a critical internal application because downtime would affect production. The vulnerability is exploitable only from a restricted subnet.

Which response BEST balances security and operations?
options: Accept the risk permanently because the subnet is restricted
Apply compensating controls, restrict access further, monitor closely, and schedule remediation
Remove all network segmentation because it creates false confidence
Mark the vulnerability as resolved because production downtime is unacceptable
